title: Consolidate header into single unified bar with proper spacing

requirements.summary: Merge the two separate header sections (tagline bar and navigation bar) into a single unified, cohesive header bar preserving all existing content
acceptanceCriteria: The header renders as a single horizontal bar with no visible second bar or sub-bar
The logo/icon, tagline text, and navigation links are all present in the unified header
The header does not visually appear as two separate sections

file_operations.description: Restructure the Header component to render as a single unified bar instead of two stacked sections. Combine the tagline bar (with logo and tagline text) and navigation bar into one horizontal container. Preserve the logo, tagline span text, desktop navigation links, and mobile hamburger menu. Keep the existing useScrollDirection hook integration and className-based hide/show behavior. Ensure all elements are arranged horizontally with proper spacing using flexbox or grid layout.

requirements.summary: Ensure the unified header does not overlap or block any page content below it
acceptanceCriteria: No page content is visually hidden or overlapped by the header
The hero section begins below the full height of the header with visible separation

file_operations.description: Review and adjust the header's positioning strategy to prevent content overlap. If the header uses fixed or sticky positioning, ensure the body or main container has adequate top padding or margin to account for the header's height. Add appropriate spacing utilities or inline styles to push content below the header bar.

Add top padding or margin to the main content container (or directly to the HeroSection wrapper) to provide clearance below the header. Ensure the spacing accounts for the unified header's full height so no content is hidden behind it when the page loads.

requirements.summary: Retain the existing hide-on-scroll-down behavior for the unified header
acceptanceCriteria: The header hides (slides up or fades out) when the user scrolls down
The header reappears when the user scrolls up
Behavior matches the existing scroll-direction logic

file_operations.description: Verify that the unified header layout preserves the existing useScrollDirection hook integration. Ensure the conditional className logic that applies hide/show transitions based on scroll direction remains intact after restructuring the header. Test that the scroll behavior (hiding on scroll-down, reappearing on scroll-up) works seamlessly with the new single-bar layout.

requirements.summary: Add sufficient top margin or padding-top to the hero section so the hero image is not too close to the header
acceptanceCriteria: The hero image has a noticeable gap below the header and does not feel cramped against it
The spacing looks intentional and consistent with the site's visual style

file_operations.description: Add top padding or margin to the HeroSection component to create a comfortable visual gap between the bottom of the unified header and the top of the hero image. Use Tailwind spacing utilities (e.g., pt-24, pt-32, or custom values) to ensure the hero content does not feel cramped against the header. The spacing should feel intentional and match the site's overall design aesthetic.
